title: Gwellib Ap-Llewff
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:BorisKarloffApLlewff-DETrilogy.png]] |name=Gwellib Ap-Llewff |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|White]][[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]] |feathers= |eyes=White |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' **[[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]''[[Evasive Action: Recruitment]]'' **[[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]] *[[Dark Empire]] **[[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]] |masters=[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] {{C|[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] master}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You are an extension of my will.|Darth Vader, to Ap-Llewff and the other Inquisitors|}} '''Gwellib Ap-Llewff''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] and [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Originally a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], he was not selected to become a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], instead joining the [[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]]. He was part of a group of AgriCorps members and other [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] captured and kept in captivity on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] shortly after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], but was freed when [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] selected him and three others to become his first Inquisitors. Ap-Llewff was taken to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with the others and was taught the art of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] and other [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] abilities by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Lord]] Vader, though he was often bested by another trainee, [[Antinnis Tremayne]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Ap-Llewff and his fellow Inquisitors failed to capture fugitive Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Drake Lo'gaan/Legends|"Jodd Sonta,"]] though they did not report their failure to Vader. Eventually, Ap-Llewff returned to Byss to serve as one of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] Dark Side Adepts, and was stationed alongside [[Savuud Thimram]] at the entrance to the [[Emperor's Citadel]]. He was still on Byss with the Reborn Emperor in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]; he and Thimram taunted [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] as he attempted to gain an audience with Palpatine. ==Biography== ===Inquisitor=== {{Quote|This is a private transport. We were just exiting.''"
"''I'm afraid we can't let you do that, young lady.|[[Ekria/Legends|Ekria]] and Gwellib Ap-Llewff|}} A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] with a short, snout-like nose, Gwellib Ap-Llewff was a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. However, he wasn't selected to become a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] learner; instead, he joined the ranks of the [[Agricultural Corps/Legends|Agricultural Corps]]. He was one of over a hundred Force-sensitives—made up of former AgriCorps members and others kidnapped over a duration of several years—kept on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and his aide, [[Sly Moore/Legends|Sly Moore]], during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. They were slowly turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and made absolutely loyal to Sidious. When the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] public persona, Palpatine, dissolved the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and created the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], he told his apprentice, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], to select several of the recruits to become "extensions of his will." Vader arrived in the hall where Ap-Llewff and the other recruits were training and mocked those who had desired to become [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. He [[Telepathy/Legends|Force choked]] several of the students, though Ap-Llewff and three others—[[Antinnis Tremayne|Tremayne]], [[Halmere]], and [[Lanu Pasiq]]—fended him off. Using crude sticks, they attacked Vader and kept the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] at bay. Impressed, Vader took the four to become his dark-side agents. [[File:Inquisitortraining.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ap-Llewff, training with his fellow Inquisitors]] The four dark-siders were inducted into a new organization, the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitorius]], which reported directly to Vader and the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. The Inquisitorius was tasked with destroying what was left of the Old Republic, including the few Jedi who had survived [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. They were taken to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with Vader to speak to a gathering of members of the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] [[Sub-Adult Group/Legends|Sub-Adult Group]]. Ap-Llewff and Lanu Pasiq looked on as Tremayne made his speech, praising Vader and the Empire. After the speech, Vader took his four Inquisitors to watch them [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]] each other with [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and begin their training. During the fight, Tremayne incapacitated Pasiq and Halmere and took their lightsabers, leaving Ap-Llewff and Tremayne to face off. Ap-Llewff, not wishing to duel Tremayne's three weapons, surrendered, and Vader left them. Tremayne was sent to hunt down several rogue Jedi while the others continued their training, though Ap-Llewff, Halmere, and Pasiq soon had to assist their fellow Inquisitor. They forced entry into a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] on Coruscant where Tremayne had been bested by [[Drake Lo'gaan/Legends|"Jodd Sonta,"]] a fugitive Jedi. The three Inquisitors were met with [[Zonder/Legends|Zonder]], a [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] Jedi, and several other of Sonta's affiliates. They ordered Sonta's allies to return to their cabins—bar Zonder, whom they wanted to take back to Vader. The Inquisitors were not expecting an attack and were not armed with their lightsabers; Ap-Llewff and his peers were attacked and defeated by the unarmed criminals, bound together, and left on the ship as the Jedi and their allies fled. Eventually, Ap-Llewff was released and returned to an Imperial facility, but Tremayne, who had become the ''de facto'' leader of the four, strictly forbade them to tell Vader of their failure. While Tremayne and Lanu Pasiq were sent after "Jodd Sonta,"''[[Evasive Action: Prey]]'' Ap-Llewff and Halmere were sent to hunt down the Jedi elsewhere in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].{{Blog|ghent|63/comments|Name That Inquisitor}} ===Dark Side Adept=== {{Quote|Let it be his 'free choice,' if that's what he wants to believe…but he's walking to his doom!|Savuud Thimram, speaking of Luke Skywalker, to Gwellib Ap-Llewff|}} [[File:Inquisitorgwellib.jpg|thumb|right|Gwellib Ap-Llewff]] Eventually, however, Ap-Llewff was transferred permanently to Byss to become one of the Emperor's [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adepts]]. These Adepts were hand picked by Palpatine because of their abilities and their loyalty, and they performed whatever tasks he required of them; Ap-Llewff and another Dark Side Adept, [[Savuud Thimram]], were often stationed at the entrance to Palpatine's [[Emperor's Citadel|Imperial Citadel]]. While serving on Byss, Ap-Llewff took to wearing ceremonial dress from his native planet and carrying a jeweled walking stick, though he also carried his lightsaber with him at all times. Palpatine's minions, including Ap-Llewff, used Byss's natural dark-side connection to further their powers, and they fed off the life forces of the entire populace of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]]. After Palpatine's [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|first death]] at the hands of Darth Vader—who had been turned against his Master by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Emperor's spirit traveled to Byss to inhabit a [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] body. After several years, he and his loyal Imperials launched an attack on the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], finally revealing the Emperor's revival. Luke Skywalker, now a self-proclaimed [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], decided that the only way to defeat the Reborn Emperor would be to travel to Byss and attempt to defeat the dark side from within. Ap-Llewff and Thimram were waiting for him. When Skywalker arrived at the Imperial Citadel in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Ap-Llewff, Thimram, and several other Dark Side Adepts watched as the Jedi pushed aside the Citadel guards with ease; they found the altercation quite amusing. Thimram ordered his companion to keep a close eye on Skywalker as he advanced towards Palpatine's position, though they decided not to attack him and take him to Palpatine personally—it was all the same to them whether he went with an escort or of his own free will. Believing that Skywalker was walking towards his peril, Ap-Llewff and his fellows mocked him with raucous laughter. However, Palpatine was eventually defeated, and Byss was left a smoking rubble after being attacked by the [[Galaxy Gun]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. The planet took all its inhabitants with it to the grave.''[[Star Wars: Empire's End]]'' ==Personality and traits== [[File:Gwellib_side.png|thumb|left|Inquisitor Gwellib Ap-Llewff]] Gwellib Ap-Llewff was loyal to the Imperial cause and spent the majority of his life serving others—Vader, Tremayne, Palpatine, and Thimram among them. He rarely spoke, letting others direct and guide him. Although determined, Ap-Llewff knew when to give up, and during his initial sparring lessons he surrendered rather than fight with Tremayne. Ap-Llewff and Thimram both found Luke Skywalker's plight highly amusing, and they mocked and jeered him as he strode past them toward Palpatine. Ap-Llewff was a member of an unidentified [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]], with a large, protruding, snout-like nose, which continued to grow as he aged. Although he had a thin build as a youth, by 10 ABY Ap-Llewff had grown in stature and was considerably taller than Thimram, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] male.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planet2|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 2: Empress Teta}} While he was an Inquisitor, Ap-Llewff wore the traditional robes of the order, though when serving Palpatine on Byss he often carried a ceremonial, jewel-crusted walking stick and wore a traditional ceremonial dress from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]; many different species were represented in the Dark Side Adepts, and Byss was not mired with [[Humanocentrism]] as was Coruscant. ==Powers and abilities== [[File:Gwellib_saber.jpg|thumb|right|190px|Gwellib Ap-Llewff, brandishing his lightsaber on Coruscant]] Gwellib Ap-Llewff was trained in the ways of the dark side of the Force by both Palpatine and Vader, leaving him with lightsaber combat abilities and myriad [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]]. However, Ap-Llewff's talents with a [[Gwellib Ap-Llewff's lightsaber|lightsaber]] were less than those of Antinnis Tremayne, and he was defeated in a duel by the Human early in his Imperial career. Whether he improved as a duellist afterwards remains a mystery. Ap-Llewff, Lanu Pasiq, and Halmere were also easily outmaneuvered by the Jedi Zonder and his allies in 19 BBY, when the Inquisitors opted to use [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] instead of their lightsabers. Later, while serving as the Emperor's Dark Side Adept, Ap-Llewff was able to use many Force abilities taught to him by Palpatine, though he always carried his lightsaber with him. He could use the Force to [[Force healing/Legends|accerlate healing]], control pain, enhance attributes, resist stuns, and to sense attack, sense danger or sense life. He was capable on feeding on the Dark Side, and inflicting pain through the Force. He could also [[Mind trick/Legends|control other beings' minds]], enhance coordination, and of course, [[Telekinesis/Legends|Telekinesis]], among other abilities. ==Behind the scenes== Gwellib Ap-Llewff first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 2|second issue]] of [[Tom Veitch]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' series, where he was illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. He appeared only on one page and was not named, though [[Michael Allen Horne]]'s ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' provided his name and also listed [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] statistics. He was later reused by [[Paul Ens]] in his webstrip, ''[[Evasive Action: Recruitment]]'', drawn by [[Tom Hodges|Thomas Hodges]], in [[2005]], though he was only identified as Ap-Llewff in Paul Ens's blog. In [[2008]] he received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which established that he was a member of the AgriCorps, as was implied by ''Evasive Action: Recruitment''. His ''Encyclopedia'' entry was later republished on the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]]. Both entries misspell his surname as "Ap-Llewf."{{DB|character|gwellibapllewf|Ap-Llewf, Gwellib|nobackup=1}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Gwellib Ap-Llewff}} *''[[Evasive Action: Recruitment]]'' *[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' {{1stID|Gwellib Ap-Llewff}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|gwellibapllewf|Ap-Llewf, Gwellib|nobackup=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Gwellib Ap-Llewff |pt=Gwellib Ap-Llewff }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ap-Llewff, Gwellib}} [[Category:Dark side adepts]] [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Individuals of unidentified species]] [[Category:Inquisitors]] [[Category:Jedi Purge survivors]] [[Category:Jedi trainees of the Jedi Order]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Personnel of the Dark Empire]]
